title: Nearer, My God, To Thee

w: 5.~But to be near- er still, Bring me, O God,  *

w: E'en though it be a cross that * rais- eth me,  *

w: Who with my lo- ving Lord Dwel- * lest with me!  *

w: Still all my song shall be, near- er, my God, to Thee. 
w: Yet in my dreams I'd be near- er, my God to Thee. 
w: Streng- then my wil- ling feet, Hold me in ser- vice sweet 
w: Grant me thy fel- low- ship! Help me each day to keep 
w: Here where thy cross I see, Je- sus, I wait for thee, 
[V: S1V2]  G3 G2 G | G2 G G3 | G3 G2 G | D2 D D3 |
[V: S2V1]  B,3 C2 B, | B,2 G, B,3 | B,3 C2 B, | A,2 G, F,3 |

w: Near- er, my God, to Thee, Near- * er to Thee!  * 
w: 5.~Then ev- er- more to be Near- * er to Thee!  *

% 18
%%vskip -0.4cm
W:6.There in my Father's home, safe and at rest, 
W:There in my Savior's love, perfectly blest; Age
W:after age to be, nearer my God to Thee.

metadata.lyricist: st. 1-2 by Sarah F. Adams, 1841. st. 3-5 by Hervey D. Ganse (1822-1891) st. 6 by Edward H. Bickersteth, Jr. (1825-1906).
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2008 Revision.
c_lines: Words: st. 1-2 by Sarah F. Adams, 1841. st. 3-5 by Hervey D. Ganse (1822-1891).
st. 6 by Edward H. Bickersteth, Jr. (1825-1906).
Music: 'Bethany (Mason)' Lowell Mason, 1856.  Setting: Lowell Mason, 1859.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2008 Revision.

1.raw_text: Nearer, my God, to Thee, nearer to Thee! E'en though it be a cross that raiseth me, Still all my song shall be, nearer, my God, to Thee. Nearer, my God, to Thee, Nearer to Thee!

2.raw_text: Though like the wanderer, the sun gone down, Darkness be over me, my rest a stone. Yet in my dreams I'd be nearer, my God to Thee. Then evermore to be Nearer to Thee!

3.raw_text: Nearer, my Lord, to thee, Nearer to thee! Who to the cross didst come, Dying for me! Strengthen my willing feet, Hold me in service sweet

4.raw_text: Nearer, O Comforter, Nearer to thee! Who with my loving Lord Dwellest with me! Grant me thy fellowship! Help me each day to keep

5.raw_text: But to be nearer still, Bring me, O God, Not by the visioned steeps Angels have trod. Here where thy cross I see, Jesus, I wait for thee,
